users.bio: Media systems operator and guest on Episode 14.
Practical systems for small creator teams.
Metadata and RSS tooling for publishers.
Keyboard sound design and desk setup reviews.
Macro market commentary and weekly charts.
Indie game soundtrack clips and composer notes.
Home gardening and balcony herb experiments.

posts.text: Small podcast teams do not need more AI. They need fewer invisible handoffs. If your transcript, description, and clips all live in different places, publishing quality becomes luck.
@maya_chen Exactly. Most workflow debt is really state-management debt.
Metadata drift is one of the quietest ways to degrade a show. Your title, description, artwork, and chapters have to agree.
Rates, volatility, and positioning all moved before breakfast. Busy macro morning.
Swapped my desk monitors again. Still chasing the perfect nearfield setup for synth practice.
Layered strings plus tape hiss is still undefeated for moody menu music.
My basil recovered faster after I moved it away from the afternoon window.

Use automation to accelerate repetitive transforms — transcription cleanup, first-draft notes, formatting, asset packaging. But never automate the approval step. Someone still has to own correctness, tone, and release quality before anything goes public. Ep. 14 out now.
At what point in your production workflow does a raw transcript become trustworthy enough to build on? Ep. 14 digs into where that line sits — and what breaks when teams skip the review step.

direct_messages.text: Happy to boost the episode once it's live.
